economic_notes: Band has investments in 7 government business enterprises including Kalum Quarry, Kalum Gas Bar, Kitsumkalum Development LP, Kalum Ventures LP, Kalum Logistics Park LP, Kitsumkalum Economic Development Group Ltd, and Kalum Ventures Ltd. Port of Prince Rupert Container Terminal Project Agreement trust fund for economic development. Purchased woodlot license for $200,000.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED because the Band did not prepare an approved budget for the year ended March 31, 2022, as required by Canadian public sector accounting standards. Three entities consolidated (Kitsumkalum Treaty Administration, Kitsumkalum Social Housing, House of Sim-Oi-Ghets). Seven GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. Large CMHC revenue of $3,525,338 in 2022 vs $209,629 in 2021 due to rapid housing funding now recognized. ISC deferred revenue movement of $4.8M reduced recognized ISC revenue significantly from $13.7M confirmed to $8.8M recognized. Net financial assets are very strong at $48.7M. Financial statements dated July 24, 2023 for March 31, 2022 year-end indicating significant delay.
